title: Diag: tighter inter-frame timing sweep
description: The degradation diagnostic used 0.5-1.0s gaps between frames, but the real HTTPS ARQ loop has much shorter gaps (~50-100ms turnaround). Add a phase to diag_vac_degradation.py (or a new script) that sends 20 frames with progressively tighter inter-frame delays: 1.0s, 0.5s, 0.25s, 0.1s, 0.05s. Find the timing threshold where demod quality degrades. This isolates whether the failure is purely a timing/settling issue in PortAudio or the VAC driver.
close_reason: T1 timing sweep: 100/100 PASS at all delays (1.0s down to 0.05s). No timing-dependent degradation.

title: Add configurable baud rate (300/1200)
description: Make baud rate configurable via --baud-rate CLI flag and MODEM_BAUD_RATE env var. Default stays 300. Wire through ProxyConfig, Modem, transport timeouts, and CLI.
close_reason: Implemented configurable baud rate across all layers

title: Diag: ARQ loop simulation over VAC
description: The raw modem diagnostic sends one-way DATA frames. Real HTTPS traffic uses bidirectional ARQ: DATA -> ACK -> DATA -> ACK with stop-and-wait timing. Create a test that simulates the full ARQ exchange pattern (proxy sends DATA, relay sends ACK back, repeat) for 20+ round trips at 1200 baud full-duplex. This is the closest reproduction of the real protocol without involving HTTP/TLS layers. Compare per-frame metrics to the one-way diagnostic to see if the ACK-interleaved pattern triggers degradation.
close_reason: T2 ARQ simulation: 40/40 PASS (20 DATA + 20 ACK round trips). ARQ loop itself does not cause degradation.

title: Add real acoustic speaker-to-mic e2e test
description: The e2e test uses virtual audio cables. Add a test mode that works over real speakers and microphones (acoustic profile) to validate the full air-gap scenario that modumb is designed for.

title: HTTPS E2E fails at seq=10: TX/RX collision on separate VAC cables
description: After fixing the DFT offset alignment bug (all-zero payload demod), HTTPS E2E tests now pass through seq=9 but fail at seq=10. Both proxy and relay transmit simultaneously on separate VAC cables, causing CRC corruption (received=0x0000) on the relay side. Happens in both half-duplex and full-duplex modes. The relay successfully demodulates preamble/sync/header but the last bytes (CRC) are corrupted, suggesting concurrent TX/RX on separate VAC devices causes PortAudio buffer issues at capture edges.

title: Explore higher baud rates for faster transfers
description: Current 300 baud rate means ~89 seconds for a simple example.com fetch. Explore 600 or 1200 baud over virtual audio cables where clock drift is zero. May need adaptive baud rate negotiation during session handshake.
close_reason: 1200 baud fully implemented: AFSK, timeouts, CLI flags, config, and E2E tests all in place. Original work closed as modumb-1hv.

title: Fix SYN decode failures during session startup
description: During e2e tests, the first 1-2 SYN frames fail to decode (189 bytes of garbage) because the relay's accept() hasn't fully started listening before the proxy sends SYN. ARQ retries recover, but this wastes ~10-15 seconds. The relay subprocess needs time to initialize audio streams before the proxy starts transmitting. May need a ready signal or longer startup delay.

title: Add individual E2E test selection for Jenkins
description: Replace RUN_FULL_MATRIX boolean with E2E_TESTS string parameter. Add run action to fetch_jenkins.ps1 for triggering specific tests by ID or preset.
close_reason: Implemented E2E_TESTS param in Jenkinsfile, run action in fetch_jenkins.ps1, updated CLAUDE.md docs

title: Fix SYN decode failures in e2e tests
description: Add on_ready callback + RELAY READY marker to avoid lost SYN frames
close_reason: on_ready callback + RELAY READY marker working, zero SYN retries in e2e tests

title: Diag: TLS payload byte pattern impact on demod
description: TLS handshake payloads (ClientHello, ServerHello, Certificate) have different byte distributions than the fixed test payload (b'DIAG' + range(60)). TLS records are heavy on 0x00-0x03 bytes and structured fields, which may produce mark-biased AFSK signals. Capture actual TLS handshake bytes from a successful non-VAC run, then replay those exact payloads through the VAC modem diagnostic to see if mark_ratio degrades faster with TLS-like content. Also test with all-0x00 (all-mark) and all-0xFF payloads as extremes.
close_reason: T3 payload patterns: all_zero was failing 8/9 frames due to DFT offset alignment bug. Fixed in 8e56722 — DFT now finds its own optimal offset. 8/8 saved WAVs decode correctly, T3 rerun shows all_zero PASS.

title: Diag: session handshake warmup effect
description: Real HTTPS tests start with SYN/SYN-ACK/ACK handshake frames (small, no payload) before DATA frames begin. The handshake may alter VAC driver or PortAudio internal state differently than jumping straight to DATA. Add a test variant that sends SYN, waits for SYN-ACK, sends ACK, then proceeds with 20 DATA frames. Compare to Phase 3 (alternating DATA-only) to isolate whether the handshake sequence contributes to later degradation.

title: Diag: RX queue accumulation during sustained full-duplex
description: In full-duplex mode, the InputStream callback continuously queues blocks to _rx_queue even during TX. After multiple TX/RX cycles, the queue may accumulate stale audio blocks that shift the demodulator's view of the signal. Add a test that monitors _rx_queue depth (rx_queue.qsize()) before each receive() call across a 20-frame bidirectional exchange. If queue depth grows over time, it indicates the RX pipeline isn't draining fast enough and receive_until_silence() is demodulating a mix of stale and fresh audio.

title: Add Jenkins CI/CD pipeline
description: Create Jenkinsfile for automated unit tests and serialized E2E VAC tests. Uses lock('vac-audio-devices') to prevent concurrent VAC access across branches.
close_reason: Jenkinsfile created with unit test, change detection, E2E smoke, and full matrix stages. test_e2e_vac.py updated to respect MODUMB_PYTHON env var.

title: Diag: reproduce degradation with plain HTTP large response
description: HTTPS fails at frame seq=8 but HTTP tests pass. HTTP tests use small/medium responses that finish in ~7 frames. If we force a plain HTTP response large enough to exceed 8 frames (e.g. 512+ bytes requiring 10-15 DATA frames at 64B/frame), does degradation appear? This isolates whether the issue is CONNECT/TLS-specific or simply a frame-count/duration threshold. Test: configure remote relay to return a large static response (or fetch a real URL with a large body), run over VAC at 1200 baud full-duplex, and monitor per-frame demod quality past frame 8. If plain HTTP with 15+ frames passes cleanly, the root cause is CONNECT tunnel or TLS-specific (bidirectional interleaving pattern, binary payload distribution, or tunnel framing overhead).

title: Move Jenkins API token out of fetch_jenkins.ps1 into env/credential store
description:
fetch_jenkins.ps1 line 1 has the Jenkins API token hardcoded in plaintext: $pair = "john:11bb5c...". This is checked into the repo and in git history.

Fix plan:
1. Move token to env var (JENKINS_API_TOKEN) - script reads from $env:JENKINS_API_TOKEN
2. Update fetch_jenkins.ps1 to fail with clear error if env var missing
3. Add permissions.deny rule to block Claude from reading any credential files
4. Rotate the Jenkins API token since it's been in git history
5. Consider adding .env to permissions.deny in .claude/settings.json as general hygiene
close_reason: Replaced hardcoded API token with JENKINS_API_TOKEN env var via WSLENV forwarding

title: HTTPS/CONNECT VAC e2e tests fail at modem layer (demodulation degradation after ~8 tunnel exchanges)
description: HTTPS e2e tests (test matrix #8, #9) consistently fail with deterministic garble pattern (005a39188b79a374...) on VAC Cable 2 after the CONNECT handshake completes. The tunnel protocol itself works correctly (CONNECT 200 is sent/received), but subsequent frames fail to demodulate. This blocks the Jenkins pipeline when HTTPS tests are included in smoke tests. Root cause appears to be AFSK demodulation degradation after extended tunnel exchanges. HTTP tests (1-7) are unaffected. HTTPS tests are currently disabled in Jenkinsfile and CLAUDE.md smoke tests.
notes:
## Attempt 1 Results (Build #8)

### What worked:
- **Bug A fix** (receive_until_silence returning empty when no signal): Eliminated the 340-byte garble pattern. Proxy now cleanly times out instead of decoding garbage.
- **Bug B fix** (noise threshold cap at 0.05): Relay's first tunnel frame (DATA seq=2) now succeeds despite noise_rms=0.2723 during noise measurement. Previously threshold=1.06 blocked all detection.
- **Transition delays** (0.5s on both sides): Helped but not sufficient alone.
- CONNECT handshake + initial tunnel frames (seq 2-7) all pass cleanly.

### What still fails:
- **Race condition at seq=8 (and later seq=10)**: Relay captures 35840 samples (747ms) on Cable 1 — correct frame size — but the relay's demodulated line appears ~5s later (after 2 proxy retries). The relay is NOT slow to demodulate; rather the relay's modem.receive() call that captured 35840 samples returns data, but the ACK doesn't reach the proxy in time.
- Root cause: half-duplex tunnel turn-taking race. After the relay sends its response (DATA seq=M) and receives ACK, both sides race to re-enter their next modem.receive(). The relay's clear_receive_buffer() + noise measurement (~100ms overhead) creates a window where the proxy's next DATA frame arrives but gets partially drained or the relay's noise measurement captures AFSK signal.
- The pattern: first HTTPS test (half-duplex) gets through seq=2-7 cleanly, fails at seq=8 with 2 retries, then collides at seq=10 with both sides sending simultaneously.

## Attempt 2 Results (this session)

### Fixes applied:
1. **modem.py**: Signal recovery — if noise probe captures AFSK signal (RMS > 0.05), re-queue samples so receive_until_silence() can use them. Added debug prints.
2. **audio_io.py**: receive_until_silence() returns empty when no signal detected (was returning noise).
3. **reliable.py**: Turnaround guard (0.1s) before ACK in half-duplex receive() to reduce race conditions.
4. **test_frame.py**: Dynamic preamble offset in CRC corruption test.

### Test results:
- **HTTP half-duplex 1200 baud: PASS** — 32.2s, 16.4 B/s, zero retransmissions, all frames through seq=12
- **HTTP full-duplex 1200 baud: PASS** — 32.4s, 19.5 B/s, zero retransmissions, all frames through seq=12
- **HTTPS full-duplex 1200 baud: FAIL at seq=8** — Relay captures 36864 samples (768ms, correct frame size) but demodulates to 0xFF×16 + garbled header. All 3 demod strategies fail. Proxy retransmits 5x, all fail identically.

### Key observation:
The demodulated output `fffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffd0720000001...` shows 16 bytes of 0xFF (all mark frequency) instead of 16 × 0xAA (alternating mark/space). The demodulator is interpreting the entire signal as mark frequency (1200 Hz). This happens on VAC Cable 1 at exactly the same point (proxy DATA seq=8) in both half-duplex and full-duplex modes. The captured sample count is correct, so the audio IS arriving — but the frequency content is wrong or the demodulator is failing to discriminate mark from space.

### Failed experiment:
- Tried increasing preamble from 16 to 24 bytes. Broke everything — _score_alignment() searches for sync up to index 24, missing sync at position 24 with longer preamble. SYN frames couldn't decode at all. Reverted.

### Next steps (Attempt 3):
- Investigate whether the VAC driver introduces frequency distortion or buffer artifacts after ~20 frame exchanges
- Try dumping raw audio samples to WAV file at the point of failure for offline analysis
- Consider whether the issue is in RMS normalization (_compute_normalized_envelopes) when the signal characteristics change mid-session
- May need to look at whether the modulator phase accumulator or TX volume creates subtle frequency shifts after many frames

title: Jenkins Check E2E Needed misses merge commits on master
description: The Check E2E Needed stage uses 'git diff --name-only HEAD~1...HEAD' for master, which only checks the last commit. For merge commits, this shows only the merge commit changes, not all files brought in by the merged branch. Fixed by detecting merge commits (HEAD^2 exists) and using HEAD^1...HEAD to diff against first parent.
close_reason: Fixed in 1ce3c1f — Jenkinsfile now detects merge commits via HEAD^2 and diffs against first parent

title: Debug HTTPS full-duplex e2e failure at 1200 baud
description: Multi-phase fix: always re-queue noise probe samples, increase ACK guard to 150ms, widen demodulator settling margin to 16 bit periods, add WAV dump diagnostics and TX/RX timing prints
close_reason: Partial fix: preamble/sync corruption resolved (settling margin + noise probe). Payload bit errors remain — demodulator clock drift issue. WAV dump enabled in Jenkins for next iteration.

title: Add sliding window ARQ to replace stop-and-wait
description: Current stop-and-wait ARQ sends one frame then waits for ACK before sending the next. For virtual cables with near-zero latency, a sliding window (e.g., Go-Back-N or Selective Repeat) could significantly improve throughput by keeping the channel busy.

title: Diag: noise probe / clear_receive_buffer interaction under load
description: modem.receive() does a 1024-sample noise probe and clear_receive_buffer() before each frame. In full-duplex with tight timing, the noise probe can capture part of an incoming frame preamble, and clear_receive_buffer() can discard signal samples. Under sustained bidirectional traffic the probability of this race increases. Add instrumentation to log noise_rms values and whether the probe captured signal (rms > NOISE_SIGNAL_THRESHOLD) across a 20-frame ARQ exchange. If noise probes frequently capture signal at frame 7-8+, this explains the degradation.

title: Test e2e proxy with larger HTTP responses
description: The e2e test only fetches example.com (528 bytes). Test with larger pages to stress the protocol, expose reliability issues with many sequential frames, and verify ARQ handles sustained transfers.
close_reason: Tested with info.cern.ch (646 bytes, 14 DATA frames). Zero decode failures during transfer. 6.0 bytes/sec effective throughput at 300 baud.

title: Rename test_e2e_vbcable.py to test_e2e_vac.py
description: The e2e test script is still named test_e2e_vbcable.py but now uses two Virtual Audio Cable (Muzychenko) cables instead of VB-Cable. Rename for accuracy.
close_reason: Renamed to test_e2e_vac.py, updated references in diag_modem_exchange.py

title: Explore advanced modulation for higher throughput (QAM/OFDM/PSK)
description: Current AFSK (Bell 202, 2-FSK) tops out at 1200 baud. To push throughput higher, explore more modern modulation techniques: PSK (V.22bis, 2400 bps), QAM (V.34, 28.8k), OFDM (multiple parallel carriers). Could also explore multi-tone approaches. This is a research/exploration issue to evaluate feasibility over acoustic and cable links.

title: Investigate occasional frame decode failure during e2e test
description: During the e2e proxy test, the relay hit 'Failed to decode 189 bytes' on the first DATA frame. ARQ retry recovered, but this suggests the relay occasionally picks up extra noise before the real frame. The receive pipeline may need further hardening to discard pre-frame garbage more reliably.
close_reason: Fixed: added silence drain after OutputStream.write() and 0.5s post-handshake delay. Root cause was Pa_WriteStream returning before audio fully played.

title: VAC test lock system
description: File-based cross-process lock to serialize VAC test access
close_reason: VAC lock implemented and tested (11 unit tests passing)

title: Add HTTPS/CONNECT tunnel support
description: LocalProxy currently returns 501 for CONNECT method (used by HTTPS). Implement CONNECT tunneling to support HTTPS sites through the modem proxy. This is critical for real-world usability since most sites use HTTPS.
close_reason: HTTPS CONNECT tunneling implemented with length-prefixed chunk protocol

title: Fix worktree path isolation (venv, CLAUDE.md, settings)
description: Claude Code --worktree sessions use main repo paths instead of worktree paths, causing wrong source imports and wrong scripts to run. Three sub-problems: (1) Shared .venv editable install .pth hardcodes whichever worktree last ran pip install -e, so Python imports wrong source. Fix: use PYTHONPATH=<worktree>/src instead of relying on editable install. (2) CLAUDE.md uses absolute paths like PY=.../modumb/.venv/... and $PY C:/Users/John/modumb/scripts/.... Fix: keep PY pointing to shared venv (fine) but use relative script paths (scripts/... not C:/.../scripts/...). (3) .claude/settings.json Bash allowlist has hardcoded main repo paths. May need wildcard patterns. Optional: add WorktreeCreate or SessionStart hook to auto-set PYTHONPATH and generate CLAUDE.local.md.

comments.author: John Popplewell
comments.text: Updated CLAUDE.md to set PYTHONPATH per worktree, switched script paths to relative, and made Jenkins PowerShell examples use wslpath-based worktree paths. Updated .claude/settings.json allowlist for PYTHONPATH-prefixed python, wslpath, and worktree-based fetch_jenkins.ps1 paths.

comments.author: John Popplewell
comments.text: Added direnv-based per-worktree venv automation: new .envrc sets PY/PYTHONPATH and bootstraps a local .venv via scripts/bootstrap_worktree.sh (Windows python). Updated CLAUDE.md accordingly and expanded .claude/settings.json allowlist for direnv/bootstrap.

title: Add opt-in full-duplex mode for cable/loopback
description: Implemented --duplex full|half CLI flag. When full, skips echo suppression, TX/RX locks, and turnaround delays in audio_io, modem, framer, and reliable transport. Threaded through session manager, HTTP server, local proxy, and remote relay. CLAUDE.md and e2e test script updated.
close_reason: Implemented: --duplex full|half flag across all layers, unit tests pass
